title: retrieve_similar_notes
description: Vector-only nearest-neighbor lookup against the chunk embedding index. Use this during ingestion to dedup-check a new note candidate before save_note. This is intentionally narrower than search_notes: no BM25, no graph boost, no rerank — it is fast and meant for the 'is this a near-duplicate of something we already have?' question. Result (not modeled here for v1): an array of {note_path, title, score, top_chunk_text, heading_path} ordered by descending cosine similarity.

text.description: The text to embed and search by. Typically the body_markdown of the in-flight ingest candidate, or the user's pasted content, or a concatenation of title + summary. Must be non-empty.

top_k.description: How many neighbor notes to return. Defaults to 8 if omitted. Keep small (<=12) for dedup checks; larger values are wasteful since you only need to know whether anything crosses the dup threshold.
